title: University lecture excerpt
explanation:
University lecture excerpt

Listen to part of a lecture on urban planning.

Transcript

Lecturer: Today I want to focus on why some cities become more liveable than others. It is not enough to build taller buildings or wider roads. Successful cities invest in public transport, green spaces and affordable housing. They also encourage mixed neighbourhoods where people live, work and study close together. Without this balance, cities may grow economically but still fail to improve everyday life for residents.

questions.text: The lecturer says taller buildings alone are [*not enough|the main solution|too expensive].
questions.feedback: It is not enough to build taller buildings.

questions.text: Mixed neighbourhoods combine living, working and [*studying|shopping only|tourism].
questions.feedback: Live, work and study close together.

questions.text: Some cities grow economically but fail to improve [*everyday life|international trade|weather conditions].

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The topic is urban ____.  | *planning | farming | painting
Green spaces are presented as an important ____.  | *investment | competition | sentence
The lecturer criticises cities that grow but remain ____.  | *unliveable | silent | private
Public ____ is one key factor. | *transport | debate | recipe
The speaker's tone is [*academic and analytical|emotional and angry|casual and humorous].
The main message is that growth must be [*balanced|avoided|secret].
The lecture would interest [*city planners and students|only doctors|only artists].
The lecturer implies design affects [*quality of life|only business profits|only tourism].
